title: 🎮 Game Bible - Arlen Tharys: Staff of Eldoria - Game Node System Lvl 1-100

nodes.title: @{Level 01-05}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Start in @{Home Base}; learn @{Unlock Spark}, claim @{Item Starting Staff}, meet @{Mentor}. Core goal: prove failure is a playable starting point, not a weakness.

nodes.title: @{Level 06-10}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Cross @{Zone Road}; introduce @{Mechanic Stealth}, first @{Enemy Shadow-Skitters}, and small healing interactions with broken roadside shrines.

nodes.title: @{Level 11-15}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Enter @{Zone Peaks}; begin vertical routes, wind puzzles, falling rock timing, and the first pressure tests from @{Faction Slyph Court}.

nodes.title: @{Level 16-20}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Clear @{Zone Slyph Labyrinth}; earn @{Item Sunstone}, unlock @{Unlock Sunflare}, and make @{NPC Pyria} a conditional guide.

nodes.title: @{Level 21-25}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Descend toward @{Zone Shadowfen}; new fog tiles, poison pools, false paths, and the first major optional NPC rescue chain.

nodes.title: @{Level 26-30}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Survive @{Zone Shadowfen}; face @{Boss Gloommaw}, unlock @{Unlock Mist Listening}, and recover safe routes through cursed fog.

nodes.title: @{Level 31-35}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Reach @{Zone Moonpetal Hollow}; claim @{Item Moonpetal}, unlock @{Unlock Moonveil}, and open stealth-night puzzle routes.

nodes.title: @{Level 36-40}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Turn side systems on: lantern network, mire villagers, escort shortcuts, first multi-step side-sidequests feeding into final ally flags.

nodes.title: @{Level 41-45}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Enter @{Zone Sunken Forest}; moving trees, memory illusions, root bridges, and serious doubt hallucinations built around @{Mechanic Resonance}.

nodes.title: @{Level 46-50}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Deep @{Zone Rootmaze}; meet @{NPC Nessa}, learn @{Unlock Rootbind}, and use plant-control puzzles to open noncombat paths.

nodes.title: @{Level 51-55}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Claim @{Item Earthsong}; defeat @{Boss Mirror Doubt}; unlock @{Unlock Earthpulse} and complete the triad of sun, moon and earth.

nodes.title: @{Level 56-60}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Escape into @{Zone Ash Plains}; @{Main Villain} knows where @{Protagonist} is. Add chase pressure, convoy routes and survival scouting.

nodes.title: @{Level 61-65}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Build alliances at @{Zone Warden Camp}; recruit @{NPC Lyra}, @{NPC Jun}, and faction support through sidequest outcomes.

nodes.title: @{Level 66-70}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Assault prep: gather keys, banners, veils and maps. @{Mechanic Ally Flags} decides which gates, shortcuts and boss assists unlock.

nodes.title: @{Level 71-75}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Breach @{Zone Citadel Gates}; tactical siege with few units but massive spectacle through sky effects, drums and construct patrols.

nodes.title: @{Level 76-80}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Explore @{Zone Inner Citadel}; rotate rooms, anti-magic puzzles, lore about @{Main Villain}, and choices that test mercy vs control.

nodes.title: @{Level 81-85}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Fight @{Boss Null Architect}; unlock @{Unlock Citadel Breach}; expose the machinery that converts living memory into blight.

nodes.title: @{Level 86-90}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Confront @{Boss Valerius Echo}; choose how much truth to reveal. This locks some finale dialogue and affects @{Ending Truth}.

nodes.title: @{Level 91-95}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Begin @{Final Ritual}; all relics, NPC saves, side-sidequests and level-gates orbit the staff in @{UI Staff Rings}.

nodes.title: @{Level 96-100}: Progression Band
nodes.body: Final battle in @{Zone Heart Chamber}; defeat @{Final Boss}, choose an ending, then unlock @{Postgame} and possibly @{Unlock NG Plus}.

nodes.title: Act 0: Failure Becomes Input
nodes.body: The opening does not make @{Protagonist} look powerful. It teaches that failed sparks, shaking hands and empathy are systems. @{Quest 00} turns emotional weakness into the first readable mechanic: @{Unlock Empathy Cast}.

nodes.title: Core Premise: @{Protagonist} and @{Item Core Relic}
nodes.body: @{Eldoria} is about @{Protagonist}, a terrified apprentice whose fear becomes the exact frequency the @{Item Core Relic} needs. @{Main Villain} uses @{Main Curse} to force @{World} into perfect, lifeless order; the player restores messy, living harmony through quests, choices and relics.

nodes.title: Act I: Leaving the Safe Room
nodes.body: @{Quest 01} sends @{Protagonist} from @{Home Base} into @{Zone Road}. The player learns that staying safe equals losing ground to @{Main Curse}; every step outward creates small pockets of restored color.

nodes.title: Act II: The Mountain Judges Intent
nodes.body: @{Zone Peaks} and @{Zone Slyph Labyrinth} test honesty, not damage. @{NPC Pyria} and @{Faction Slyph Court} only help if the player uses listening choices, non-lethal puzzle solutions and careful movement.

nodes.title: Act III: The Fen Learns Your Fear
nodes.body: @{Zone Shadowfen} weaponizes doubt through false routes and mirror-fog. @{Item Moonpetal} teaches that stealth and compassion can be more powerful than direct fire.

nodes.title: Act IV: The Forest of the Inner Enemy
nodes.body: @{Zone Sunken Forest} turns personal doubt into playable illusions. The key set piece is not a cutscene; @{Boss Mirror Doubt} copies mistakes the player made earlier and forces growth through mechanics.

nodes.title: Act V: The Beacon Is Seen
nodes.body: After @{Item Earthsong} joins the staff, @{Main Villain} notices the resonance. The game shifts from hidden pilgrimage to hunted march across @{Zone Ash Plains}, with @{NPC Lyra} becoming the player’s battlefield mirror.

nodes.title: Act VI: Hope Needs Logistics
nodes.body: @{Zone Warden Camp} proves that saving the world is also routes, food, maps, lanterns and people. Side-sidequests decide final support, not just collectibles.

nodes.title: Act VII: Breach the Perfect Machine
nodes.body: @{Zone Citadel Gates} and @{Zone Inner Citadel} show @{Main Villain} as control made architecture. Rooms rearrange into equations; the player breaks them with messy living systems: roots, mist, sun, allies.

nodes.title: Finale: Build the Spell From the Journey
nodes.body: @{Final Ritual} is assembled from @{Item Sunstone}, @{Item Moonpetal}, @{Item Earthsong}, NPC saves, boss outcomes and quest flags. The ending is not selected from a menu; it is constructed from the player’s path.

nodes.title: Epilogue: What Grows Back
nodes.body: @{Quest 17} lets players walk through @{Zone Restored Eldoria}. Towns, letters, gardens and scars change based on @{Mechanic Choice}, saved NPCs and how much control the player rejected or accepted.

nodes.title: Zone: @{Home Base}
nodes.body: @{Home Base} is the intimate tutorial: cracked walls, failed spell benches, hidden alcove, and pressure from @{Main Curse}. It teaches inspect, pick up, fail safely, and first tag-driven lore links to @{Mentor}, @{Item Core Relic}, and @{Quest 00}.

nodes.title: Zone: @{Zone Road}
nodes.body: @{Zone Road} is a low-risk overworld strip where grey grass reacts to tiny successful casts. It introduces patrol cones, broken shrines, first @{Enemy Shadow-Skitters}, and optional rescue paths.

nodes.title: Zone: @{Zone Peaks}
nodes.body: @{Zone Peaks} is a vertical climb with wind lanes, crumbling shale, echo voices and hidden sun lenses. Navigation uses patience: reckless sprinting collapses tiles, steady routes reveal @{Zone Slyph Labyrinth}.

nodes.title: Zone: @{Zone Slyph Labyrinth}
nodes.body: @{Zone Slyph Labyrinth} is a shifting cave of crystal and wind. It tests sincerity through puzzles, not combat: mirrors repeat player choices, Slyphs move platforms, and @{Item Sunstone} waits behind a nonviolent proof.

nodes.title: Zone: @{Zone Shadowfen}
nodes.body: @{Zone Shadowfen} is a fog maze with poison pools, false reflections and sound traps. The staff light is muted here, forcing slow movement, listening, and cooperation with @{NPC Maelin}.

nodes.title: Zone: @{Zone Moonpetal Hollow}
nodes.body: @{Zone Moonpetal Hollow} is the calm center of the mire. It works as a mini-hub with silver water, safe crafting, and the first major upgrade ritual for @{Item Moonpetal}.

nodes.title: Subzone: Mire Refuge Routes
nodes.body: Tiny hut-islands around @{Zone Shadowfen}. Completing side-sidequests for @{NPC Fenn}, @{NPC Korrin} and @{Faction Mirekin} creates safe stepping-stone routes and vendor shortcuts.

nodes.title: Zone: @{Zone Sunken Forest}
nodes.body: @{Zone Sunken Forest} is a reality-bending forest where trees change places when unseen. It introduces memory traps, moving root gates, and moral illusions linked to @{Mechanic Choice}.

nodes.title: Zone: @{Zone Rootmaze}
nodes.body: @{Zone Rootmaze} is the underground heart of the forest. Living doors only open to rhythm puzzles, restored wildlife, or @{Unlock Rootbind}. It holds @{Item Earthsong}.

nodes.title: Zone: @{Zone Ash Plains}
nodes.body: @{Zone Ash Plains} is the open chase zone. The sky shows @{Main Villain} tracking @{Item Core Relic}; the player uses cover rocks, scout signals, and restored ground patches to cross without rendering huge armies.

nodes.title: Hub: @{Zone Warden Camp}
nodes.body: @{Zone Warden Camp} is the late-midgame hub: maps, siege prep, NPC letters, crafting, and ally flags. It turns sidequest completion into visible barricades, med tents and final assists.

nodes.title: Zone: @{Zone Citadel Gates}
nodes.body: @{Zone Citadel Gates} is a tactical breach: rotating black-glass doors, construct patrol grids, siege drums, and gates that respond to either @{Item Citadel Gate Key} or high mercy/truth flags.

nodes.title: Zone: @{Zone Inner Citadel}
nodes.body: @{Zone Inner Citadel} is an anti-life puzzle palace. Rooms try to alphabetize nature into perfect order; the player breaks symmetry with roots, mist, sunbeams and unpredictable allies.

nodes.title: Final Zone: @{Zone Heart Chamber}
nodes.body: @{Zone Heart Chamber} is the final arena and ritual UI space. It reacts to every tag: relics orbit, rescued NPCs project support, and corruption scars change boss phases.

nodes.title: Postgame Zone: @{Zone Restored Eldoria}
nodes.body: @{Zone Restored Eldoria} remixes earlier zones with recovered color, remaining scars, optional gardens and NG+ constellations. It makes ending consequences explorable, not just narrated.

nodes.title: Main Quest 00: @{Quest 00}
nodes.body: Tutorial failure quest in @{Home Base}. Fail a light charm, inspect ruined wood, speak to @{Mentor}, and learn that failure gives readable feedback. Rewards @{Item Starting Staff} and unlocks @{Unlock Spark}.

nodes.title: Main Quest 01: @{Quest 01}
nodes.body: Leave @{Home Base} before the wards collapse. Player chooses what to pack: @{Item Thorne Emberlens}, @{Item Ashbound Satchel}, or extra healing herbs. Opens @{Zone Road}.

nodes.title: Main Quest 02: @{Quest 02}
nodes.body: Cross @{Zone Road}; restore three tiny roadside shrine tiles, avoid @{Enemy Shadow-Skitters}, and learn @{Unlock Resonance Compass}. Leads toward @{Zone Peaks}.

nodes.title: Main Quest 03: @{Quest 03}
nodes.body: Climb @{Zone Peaks}; survive wind lanes and Slyph pranks without attacking. Honest dialogue with @{NPC Pyria} lowers hazards in @{Zone Slyph Labyrinth}.

nodes.title: Main Quest 04: @{Quest 04}
nodes.body: Complete the nonviolent Slyph trial, earn @{Item Sunstone}, upgrade @{Item Core Relic}, unlock @{Unlock Sunflare}, and light the first world-healing beacon.

nodes.title: Main Quest 05: @{Quest 05}
nodes.body: Enter @{Zone Shadowfen}. The map lies, fog repeats footsteps, and @{NPC Maelin} teaches route-listening. Unlocks low-light traversal and fog-safe camp nodes.

nodes.title: Main Quest 06: Gloommaw in the Mire
nodes.body: Face @{Boss Gloommaw} using reflected sunlight, mud tiles and timing. Victory opens @{Zone Moonpetal Hollow} and gives the first major swamp shortcut.

nodes.title: Main Quest 07: @{Quest 06}
nodes.body: Reach @{Zone Moonpetal Hollow}, recover @{Item Moonpetal}, unlock @{Unlock Moonveil}, and gain stealth routes through enemies that hunt bright magic.

nodes.title: Main Quest 08: @{Quest 07}
nodes.body: Build the lantern network with @{NPC Fenn}. This main/side hybrid teaches how optional effort changes major routes. Unlocks @{Item Lantern Core} and new camp saves.

nodes.title: Main Quest 09: @{Quest 08}
nodes.body: Enter @{Zone Sunken Forest}. The forest rearranges around fear. Use @{Mechanic Resonance} to tell real roots from memory illusions.

nodes.title: Main Quest 10: @{Unlock Rootbind} Oath
nodes.body: Help @{NPC Nessa} calm living roots in @{Zone Rootmaze}. Unlock @{Unlock Rootbind}, optional pacifist paths, and root-lift traversal.

nodes.title: Main Quest 11: @{Quest 09}
nodes.body: Claim @{Item Earthsong}; fight @{Boss Mirror Doubt}; complete staff triad with @{Item Sunstone}, @{Item Moonpetal}, and @{Item Earthsong}. Unlock @{Unlock Triad Resonance}.

nodes.title: Main Quest 12: @{Quest 10}
nodes.body: @{Main Villain} detects the staff. Flee @{Zone Sunken Forest}, cross @{Zone Ash Plains}, and use @{NPC Lyra} for scout commands during chase encounters.

nodes.title: Main Quest 13: @{Quest 11}
nodes.body: At @{Zone Warden Camp}, turn sidequest outcomes into war prep: maps, banners, veils, lanterns and rescued NPCs. Unlock @{Unlock Scout Command}.

nodes.title: Main Quest 14: Siege Inventory
nodes.body: Gather @{Item Ash Plains Map}, @{Item Citadel Gate Key}, @{Item Warden Banner} and optional truth clues. The quest checks level and ally flags before the breach.

nodes.title: Main Quest 15: @{Quest 12}
nodes.body: Breach @{Zone Citadel Gates}; choose stealth gate, root gate, moon gate or sun gate depending on artifacts and side-sidequest completions.

nodes.title: Main Quest 16: @{Quest 13}
nodes.body: Navigate @{Zone Inner Citadel}; solve control-room puzzles where perfect symmetry is a trap. Unlock lore on why @{Main Villain} began the blight.

nodes.title: Main Quest 17: @{Quest 14}
nodes.body: Defeat @{Boss Null Architect}; break the machine converting memory into @{Main Curse}; earn @{Item Valerius Null Shard} and unlock @{Unlock Citadel Breach}.

nodes.title: Main Quest 18: @{Quest 15}
nodes.body: Face @{Boss Valerius Echo}. Player chooses whether to expose, forgive, or weaponize the truth. Opens final lock variables for @{Ending Mercy}, @{Ending Power}, and @{Ending Truth}.

nodes.title: Main Quest 19: @{Quest 16}
nodes.body: Assemble @{Final Ritual} with all major relics, saved NPCs, and choices. Player manually arranges staff rings in @{UI Staff Rings}; results alter final boss phases.

nodes.title: Main Quest 20: @{Quest 17}
nodes.body: After the finale, walk through restored or scarred zones, receive letters, unlock @{Postgame}, and reveal NG+ hooks if @{Ending Secret} requirements are met.

nodes.title: Side Quest: The Last Warm Candle
nodes.body: @{NPC Fenn} asks for emberglass from @{Zone Road}. Reward: early healing rest point and a candle icon in @{UI Quest Web}. Feeds @{Ending Mercy} if completed without stealing fuel.

nodes.title: Side-Sidequest: Wick From a Dead Shrine
nodes.body: Small branch of The Last Warm Candle. Search a blighted shrine, decide whether to burn a memory note or spend rare @{Item Thorne Emberlens} oil.

nodes.title: Side Quest: Pyria’s Lost Chorus
nodes.body: @{NPC Pyria} lost three wind-notes in @{Zone Peaks}. Recover them with timing jumps. Reward: @{Item Pyria Bell}, which reduces wind hazards and helps unlock @{Ending Secret}.

nodes.title: Side-Sidequest: Lume in the Crack
nodes.body: Find @{NPC Lume} trapped in a crystal fissure. Rescue with careful route puzzles, not damage. Reward: @{Item Slyph Feather} and extra Slyph dialogue in the finale.

nodes.title: Side Quest: @{Item Mire Compass}
nodes.body: @{NPC Maelin} will trade @{Item Mire Compass} for three purified water nodes in @{Zone Shadowfen}. Adds fog pings to @{UI Corruption Map}.

nodes.title: Side-Sidequest: Names Under the Water
nodes.body: Recover name-stones from drowned homes. Each name restored lowers @{Mechanic Corruption} in the mire and changes NPC lines at @{Zone Warden Camp}.

nodes.title: Side Quest: Teeth in the Reeds
nodes.body: @{NPC Korrin} marks optional @{Enemy Bog-Horrors}. Defeating or pacifying them rewards crafting parts for @{Item Shadowfen Veil}.

nodes.title: Side Quest: Moonpetal Sisters
nodes.body: Find three lesser blooms near @{Zone Moonpetal Hollow}. Use them to craft @{Item Shadowfen Veil}, improving stealth against enemies that track sunlight.

nodes.title: Side Quest: Lantern Road Network
nodes.body: Expand @{NPC Fenn}’s lanterns from mire to plains. Unlocks fast travel only on routes the player personally restored.

nodes.title: Side-Sidequest: The Lantern That Lies
nodes.body: One lantern repeats false safe routes. Inspect with @{Unlock Mist Listening}; choose repair for mercy points or overload for power points.

nodes.title: Side Quest: @{NPC Hollow Child}
nodes.body: @{NPC Hollow Child} appears as a small blight creature in @{Zone Sunken Forest}. Spare, comfort, or destroy it. Major flag for @{Ending Secret} and final boss dialogue.

nodes.title: Side-Sidequest: A Toy Made of Bark
nodes.body: Find the child’s lost bark-toy in a memory loop. Tiny emotional quest that unlocks a noncombat opening in @{Boss Mirror Doubt}.

nodes.title: Side Quest: Nessa’s Sleeping Orchard
nodes.body: @{NPC Nessa} wants old orchard roots awakened. Reward: @{Item Root Charm}, root bridges, and cheaper healing plants in later hubs.

nodes.title: Side Quest: @{NPC Rurik}’s Last Gate
nodes.body: @{NPC Rurik} guards a sealed root vault. Solve endurance puzzles to earn @{Item Ironbranch Sigil}; lets Rootborn assist at @{Zone Citadel Gates}.

nodes.title: Side Quest: @{NPC Lyra}’s Scout Debt
nodes.body: @{NPC Lyra} lost scouts across @{Zone Ash Plains}. Rescue routes add scout pings, ambush warnings and final assist arrows.

nodes.title: Side-Sidequest: The Signal Nobody Heard
nodes.body: Repair one broken signal tower with @{Item Lyra Sigil}. Adds a battlefield silhouette event during the Citadel breach.

nodes.title: Side Quest: @{Item Warden Banner}
nodes.body: @{NPC Jun} wants the old banner recovered from a construct nest. Reward: @{Item Warden Banner}; boosts morale and unlocks a camp defense set piece.

nodes.title: Side Quest: Orra’s Impossible Map
nodes.body: @{NPC Orra} maps ash storms that move every rest. Reward: @{Item Ash Plains Map}, plus clear route previews on @{UI Corruption Map}.

nodes.title: Side Quest: Night of Falling Ash
nodes.body: Defend @{Zone Warden Camp} through traps, roots, moonveil and scout commands. Outcome decides how many NPCs appear in @{Final Ritual}.

nodes.title: Side Quest: Four Ways Through Black Glass
nodes.body: Optional pre-breach quest: open sun, moon, root and truth gates. Each route depends on a different relic, NPC or level band.

nodes.title: Side Quest: Names in the Perfect Cells
nodes.body: Free prisoners whose names were sorted into numbers by @{Main Villain}. Adds human voices to the final ritual chorus.

nodes.title: Side Quest: @{NPC Selvi}’s Workshop
nodes.body: @{NPC Selvi} repairs cracked staff rings using Citadel glass. Reward: safer staff-overcharge and one extra choice in @{Final Ritual}.

nodes.title: Side Quest: The First Blight Equation
nodes.body: Find the original notes of @{Main Villain}. Choosing to reveal or hide them affects @{Ending Truth} and Lyra’s trust.

nodes.title: Side Quest: The Room Where Valerius Wept
nodes.body: Optional late-game memory chamber. Shows the villain’s grief without excusing him. Unlocks a mercy dialogue branch in the final battle.

nodes.title: Side Quest: @{Item Heartseed Prism}
nodes.body: Only appears if enough mercy, truth and rescue flags are active. Craft @{Item Heartseed Prism}; required for @{Ending Secret}.

nodes.title: Postgame Side Quest: Gardens After Blight
nodes.body: Revisit zones to plant restored seeds. Rewards cosmetics, lore, and @{Unlock NG Plus} constellations tied to completed side-sidequests.

nodes.title: Playable: @{Protagonist}
nodes.body: @{Protagonist} starts with bad casting confidence but high empathy. Progression changes posture, animation, dialogue confidence and staff idle glow rather than simply adding bigger damage numbers.

nodes.title: Mentor: @{Mentor}
nodes.body: @{Mentor} gives @{Item Core Relic}, teaches the difference between command and listening, and remains a remote emotional anchor through letters, echoes and late-game truth checks.

nodes.title: Tiny NPC: @{NPC Lume}
nodes.body: @{NPC Lume} is a small optional Slyph rescue. Comedic, fragile, and useful: gives @{Item Slyph Feather} and changes wind puzzles from punishment into cooperation.

nodes.title: Guide: @{NPC Pyria}
nodes.body: @{NPC Pyria} is the first Slyph who stops mocking @{Protagonist}. She becomes a conditional wind-guide if the player completes nonviolent Slyph choices.

nodes.title: Mire Hermit: @{NPC Maelin}
nodes.body: @{NPC Maelin} lives by listening to fog. She teaches @{Unlock Mist Listening}, trades @{Item Mire Compass}, and warns that Shadowfen illusions copy the player’s doubt.

nodes.title: Bog Hunter: @{NPC Korrin}
nodes.body: @{NPC Korrin} is a practical hunter who offers aggressive solutions. His questline lets the player choose between killing monsters or curing nests.

nodes.title: Lantern-Boy: @{NPC Fenn}
nodes.body: @{NPC Fenn} carries one warm lantern through the apocalypse. His quests create the fast-travel network and emotional reason to keep restoring small places.

nodes.title: Secret NPC: @{NPC Hollow Child}
nodes.body: @{NPC Hollow Child} is a tiny blight-born creature with human memory fragments. Sparing it unlocks one of the strongest callbacks in the final boss.

nodes.title: Rootspeaker: @{NPC Nessa}
nodes.body: @{NPC Nessa} speaks for the wounded roots in @{Zone Rootmaze}. She unlocks pacifist plant routes and rewards @{Item Root Charm}.

nodes.title: Gatekeeper: @{NPC Rurik}
nodes.body: @{NPC Rurik} is an old Rootborn warden fused with living bark. His trust unlocks @{Item Ironbranch Sigil} and root-gate support during the siege.

nodes.title: Companion: @{NPC Lyra}
nodes.body: @{NPC Lyra} is a scout and skeptic who becomes the tactical companion. She unlocks @{Unlock Scout Command}, battlefield warnings and the emotional “you are the anchor” beat.

nodes.title: Warden Scout: @{NPC Jun}
nodes.body: @{NPC Jun} represents ordinary defenders. His banner quest decides whether the camp feels desperate, brave or broken before the Citadel assault.

nodes.title: Cartographer: @{NPC Orra}
nodes.body: @{NPC Orra} maps places that move. Her map upgrades turn confusing zones into readable layers without removing discovery.

nodes.title: Relic Smith: @{NPC Selvi}
nodes.body: @{NPC Selvi} repairs staff rings from black glass. She makes late-game crafting feel physical and connects @{Item Valerius Null Shard} to safe overcharge rules.

nodes.title: Villain: @{Main Villain}
nodes.body: @{Main Villain} is not just evil power; he is control as philosophy. His scenes contrast command magic against @{Unlock Empathy Cast}, making the final battle a clash of worldviews.

nodes.title: Entity: Heart of @{World}
nodes.body: The Heart of @{World} is not a person but the final responsive system. It answers player flags in @{Final Ritual} through weather, music, NPC voices and final staff behavior.

nodes.title: Faction: @{Faction Thorne Circle}
nodes.body: Hidden scholars who protected the prophecy but failed to act early enough. They provide lore, letters and guilt-driven stakes.

nodes.title: Faction: @{Faction Slyph Court}
nodes.body: Wind spirits guarding sun memory. They test sincerity and unlock alternate traversal if respected.

nodes.title: Faction: @{Faction Mirekin}
nodes.body: Survivors in @{Zone Shadowfen}; suspicious, practical, and tied to lanterns, maps and cure choices.

nodes.title: Faction: @{Faction Rootborn}
nodes.body: Ancient forest wardens connected to @{Item Earthsong}. Their support enables root shortcuts and nonviolent siege routes.

nodes.title: Faction: @{Faction Ash Wardens}
nodes.body: Last defenders on @{Zone Ash Plains}. Their strength depends on sidequest logistics rather than abstract reputation numbers.

nodes.title: Enemy Faction: @{Faction Citadel Constructs}
nodes.body: Perfect, nameless soldiers created by @{Main Villain}. They erase color, memories and irregular behavior.

nodes.title: Faction: @{Faction Last Villagers}
nodes.body: People saved through optional quests. They become the emotional chorus in the finale and the visible payoff of world healing.

nodes.title: Set Piece: Shadow-Skitter Ambush
nodes.body: First tactical encounter. The goal is not to kill fast; use light, cover and restored grass tiles to escape or disperse @{Enemy Shadow-Skitters}.

nodes.title: Trial Boss: The Slyph Circle
nodes.body: A non-lethal “boss” where the arena attacks if the player lies, boasts or uses force. Winning grants @{Item Sunstone} through sincerity and pattern reading.

nodes.title: Boss: @{Boss Gloommaw}
nodes.body: @{Boss Gloommaw} is a mud-and-vine horror. Arena uses water depth, sunlight beams and Moonpetal foreshadowing; teaches environment-first combat.

nodes.title: Mini Boss: The Mire Reflection
nodes.body: Copies recent player movement and punishes panic. Defeated by standing still, listening, then using @{Unlock Moonveil} to reveal the real path.

nodes.title: Boss: @{Boss Mirror Doubt}
nodes.body: @{Boss Mirror Doubt} speaks in Arlen’s old self-hate and copies failed casts. Victory unlocks @{Item Earthsong} by accepting imperfection instead of overpowering it.

nodes.title: Boss: @{Boss Ash Captain}
nodes.body: @{Boss Ash Captain} hunts the beacon across @{Zone Ash Plains}. Fight uses scout signals from @{NPC Lyra}, restored cover patches and convoy protection.

nodes.title: Set Piece Boss: Night of Falling Ash
nodes.body: Camp defense boss wave. Outcome depends on lanterns, maps, rescued scouts and root traps. It is a test of player preparation.

nodes.title: Boss: @{Boss Null Architect}
nodes.body: @{Boss Null Architect} turns rooms into equations. The player breaks patterns with irregular living mechanics: root, mist, sun and ally interference.

nodes.title: Boss: @{Boss Valerius Echo}
nodes.body: @{Boss Valerius Echo} debates the player mid-fight. Attacks change based on mercy, truth and power choices made across the game.

nodes.title: Final Boss: @{Final Boss}
nodes.body: Multi-phase finale in @{Zone Heart Chamber}. Phases are built from unresolved scars, saved NPCs, relic alignment, and whether the player chooses healing, control or truth.

nodes.title: Relic: @{Item Core Relic}
nodes.body: The central upgrade object. Each major relic adds a ring, color, spell school and UI layer. It stores tags for artifacts, choices and final ritual logic.

nodes.title: Item: @{Item Thorne Emberlens}
nodes.body: A tiny lens from @{Mentor}. Reveals hidden heat trails, old runes and safe candle paths. Also used in early side-sidequest tradeoffs.

nodes.title: Item: @{Item Ashbound Satchel}
nodes.body: Inventory tutorial item. Starts small, expands through NPC upgrades, and displays relic slots connected to quest progression.

nodes.title: Item: @{Item Starting Staff}
nodes.body: The first staff is intentionally plain. It supports weak @{Unlock Spark}, shows shake animations, and later becomes visually absorbed into @{Item Core Relic}.

nodes.title: Item: @{Item Pyria Bell}
nodes.body: Optional Slyph item. Rings to freeze moving wind tiles for one beat and calls @{NPC Pyria} hints in mountain/citadel wind puzzles.

nodes.title: Item: @{Item Slyph Feather}
nodes.body: Tiny optional wind relic from @{NPC Lume}. Lets the player redirect one gust puzzle per room and adds a Slyph assist flag for the finale.

nodes.title: Relic: @{Item Sunstone}
nodes.body: From @{Zone Slyph Labyrinth}. Adds gold staff ring, unlocks @{Unlock Sunflare}, burns away fear-fog, and opens sun gates.

nodes.title: Item: @{Item Mire Compass}
nodes.body: From @{NPC Maelin}. Points by vibration instead of direction; marks real safe paths in @{Zone Shadowfen} and fake paths in red on the map.

nodes.title: Relic: @{Item Moonpetal}
nodes.body: From @{Zone Moonpetal Hollow}. Adds silver staff ring, unlocks @{Unlock Moonveil}, improves stealth and reveals hidden sorrow memories.

nodes.title: Crafted Item: @{Item Shadowfen Veil}
nodes.body: Crafted from lesser blooms and Bog-Horror silk. Lets @{Protagonist} pass enemies that hunt sunlight, but weakens raw Sunflare damage while worn.

nodes.title: Item: @{Item Lantern Core}
nodes.body: From @{NPC Fenn}. Powers fast-travel lanterns, checkpoint safety, and final ritual chorus if many lanterns are restored.

nodes.title: Item: @{Item Root Charm}
nodes.body: From @{NPC Nessa}. Makes root doors respond kindly and reduces plant-enemy aggression if the player uses non-lethal options.

nodes.title: Relic: @{Item Earthsong}
nodes.body: From @{Zone Rootmaze}. Adds emerald staff ring, unlocks @{Unlock Earthpulse}, and lets restored ground become tactical cover.

nodes.title: Item: @{Item Ironbranch Sigil}
nodes.body: From @{NPC Rurik}. Opens Rootborn vaults and creates a root-gate alternative at @{Zone Citadel Gates}.

nodes.title: Item: @{Item Lyra Sigil}
nodes.body: Companion item from @{NPC Lyra}. Enables scout pings, ambush warnings, and synchronized attacks during chase and siege encounters.

nodes.title: Item: @{Item Ash Plains Map}
nodes.body: From @{NPC Orra}. Converts ashstorm movement into readable forecast layers on @{UI Corruption Map}.

nodes.title: Item: @{Item Warden Banner}
nodes.body: From @{NPC Jun}. A morale relic: changes camp visuals, defense strength and final ritual support voices.

nodes.title: Item: @{Item Citadel Gate Key}
nodes.body: Optional mechanical route through black-glass gates. Alternative to mercy/root/moon routes; useful for players who missed sidequest assists.

nodes.title: Item: @{Item Valerius Null Shard}
nodes.body: Dangerous crafting piece. Boosts late-game staff overcharge but increases @{Mechanic Corruption} unless stabilized by @{NPC Selvi}.

nodes.title: Secret Item: @{Item Heartseed Prism}
nodes.body: Crafted from mercy, truth and rescue flags. Required for @{Ending Secret}; turns the final spell into healing dialogue instead of domination.

nodes.title: Unlock: @{Unlock Empathy Cast}
nodes.body: Core casting rule: spells become stronger when the player listens to environment signals instead of forcing outputs. Drives story and mechanics.

nodes.title: Unlock: @{Unlock Spark}
nodes.body: Basic light poke used for candles, weak enemy stun and tiny restoration. Intentionally unreliable until empathy systems are learned.

nodes.title: Unlock: @{Unlock Resonance Compass}
nodes.body: Staff vibration points toward wounded places, not north. Used for exploration, hidden sidequests and invisible pathfinding.

nodes.title: Unlock: @{Unlock Slyphstep}
nodes.body: Short wind-hop over broken tiles. Requires Slyph trust or the optional @{Item Pyria Bell}.

nodes.title: Unlock: @{Unlock Sunflare}
nodes.body: Gold beam that purges fear-fog, opens sun lenses and weakens shadow enemies. Strong but noisy.

nodes.title: Unlock: @{Unlock Mist Listening}
nodes.body: Pause-and-listen mechanic that reveals fake paths, hidden NPC calls and enemy breathing in @{Zone Shadowfen}.

nodes.title: Unlock: @{Unlock Moonveil}
nodes.body: Silver stealth field. Lets player pass watchers, reveal grief memories and cool Sunflare-overheated mechanisms.

nodes.title: Unlock: Lantern Fast Travel
nodes.body: Fast travel exists only between lanterns personally restored. Makes sidequest work visible and practical.

nodes.title: Unlock: @{Unlock Rootbind}
nodes.body: Hold enemies, pull root switches, create bridges, or pacify plant threats. Strongest when used to control space rather than deal damage.

nodes.title: Unlock: @{Unlock Earthpulse}
nodes.body: Turns restored ground into cover, pressure plates, healing circles and boss weak-point triggers.

nodes.title: Unlock: @{Unlock Scout Command}
nodes.body: Companion command from @{NPC Lyra}: mark patrols, create distraction arrows, or call timed assists.

nodes.title: Unlock: Warden Camp Network
nodes.body: Combines maps, lanterns, scouts and vendors into a late-game preparation web. More completed sidequests = more siege options.

nodes.title: Unlock: @{Unlock Citadel Breach}
nodes.body: Opens Citadel gate logic. Requires level, item, or ally route. The gate UI displays missing tags to guide completion.

nodes.title: Unlock: @{Unlock Triad Resonance}
nodes.body: Sun + Moon + Earth staff state. Enables combo puzzles and final ritual construction.

nodes.title: Unlock: Safe Staff Overcharge
nodes.body: Requires @{NPC Selvi} or high relic stability. Lets the staff break anti-magic locks without corrupting the ending.

nodes.title: Unlock: @{Unlock Heartstorm}
nodes.body: Final casting interface. Player arranges relic rings, allies and choices; output determines boss phase and ending.

nodes.title: Unlock: @{Unlock NG Plus}
nodes.body: Unlocked through completion or secret ending. Adds echo routes, harder corruption, new tags and alternate NPC memory states.

nodes.title: Mechanic: @{Mechanic Grid}
nodes.body: All zones use tile states: ash, fog, root, light, water, memory, glass and restored soil. Keeps the game browser-friendly and readable.

nodes.title: Mechanic: @{Mechanic Resonance}
nodes.body: The staff is a sensor. It hums, warms, dims or pulls based on wounds, lies, gates, relics and NPC emotional states.

nodes.title: Mechanic: @{Mechanic Stealth}
nodes.body: Patrol cones, sound tiles, fog cover and decoy casts. @{Protagonist} is hunted, not overpowered, especially before level 50.

nodes.title: Mechanic: Wind / Height Traversal
nodes.body: Mountain-specific logic: wind pushes, crumbling edge tiles, Slyph platforms and timed gust lanes.

nodes.title: Mechanic: @{Mechanic Corruption}
nodes.body: Pressure meter that rises in blighted zones, when overcharging, or when using control-heavy choices. It changes enemies, music and endings.

nodes.title: Mechanic: Listening Mode
nodes.body: Brief slow-down state where ambient audio and staff pulse reveal true routes. Good for accessibility and tension.

nodes.title: Mechanic: @{Unlock Moonveil} Stealth Grammar
nodes.body: Moonveil hides light but cannot restore ground. Creates a tactical tradeoff between safety and healing.

nodes.title: Mechanic: @{Mechanic World Healing}
nodes.body: Tiles and hubs remember restoration. Lit lanterns, cured nests and spared creatures push color back on the map.

nodes.title: Mechanic: Memory Illusion Puzzles
nodes.body: Illusions use earlier player failures and choices. The solution is often emotional consistency plus mechanical observation.

nodes.title: Mechanic: Root Control
nodes.body: Rootbind, root bridges, plant pacification and environmental traps. Emphasizes battlefield shaping instead of raw attacks.

nodes.title: Mechanic: @{Mechanic Combo}
nodes.body: Simple readable combos: Sunflare + Moonveil = Eclipse Reveal; Rootbind + Earthpulse = Living Wall; Moonveil + Scout Command = Silent Flank.

nodes.title: Mechanic: @{Mechanic Ally Flags}
nodes.body: NPC and faction outcomes become final variables: routes, assists, shop inventory, boss interrupts and ending text.

nodes.title: Mechanic: Level + Item Gate Matrix
nodes.body: Every major gate can check level tags, item tags, NPC tags, or choice tags. Prevents hard lock while rewarding preparation.

nodes.title: Mechanic: @{Mechanic Boss Arenas}
nodes.body: Bosses have 2-4 clear arena rules, not giant health bars. Each teaches a lesson: listening, mercy, truth, preparation or anti-control.

nodes.title: Mechanic: @{Mechanic Choice}
nodes.body: Tracks method, not morality: heal, control, reveal, hide, spare, destroy. Final ritual weighs the pattern across the whole journey.

nodes.title: System: Tag-First Node Graph
nodes.body: All key names use tags: zones, NPCs, quests, items, levels and endings. Renaming @{NPC Lyra} or @{Zone Shadowfen} updates connected text safely.

nodes.title: System: Save State
nodes.body: Save level, zone, staff rings, inventory, quest flags, ally flags, corruption, restored tiles and ending variables.

nodes.title: System: Quest State Machine
nodes.body: Mainquests, sidequests and side-sidequests use prerequisites, outcomes, rewards and callbacks into UI, NPC dialogue and world healing.

nodes.title: System: Zone Chunk Renderer
nodes.body: 2D tile chunks stream by zone; parallax skies create scale without expensive scenes.

nodes.title: System: Lightweight Enemy AI
nodes.body: Enemies use patrols, alert states, tile priorities and boss scripts. Works well with stealth, fog and root traps.

nodes.title: System: Persistent World Healing
nodes.body: Restored lanterns, cured nests and solved shrines stay changed. The map and NPC lines read the same flags.

nodes.title: System: Companion Command Layer
nodes.body: @{NPC Lyra} commands use cooldowns, line-of-sight and trust flags. Other NPC assists can plug into the same system.

nodes.title: System: Final Ritual Calculator
nodes.body: Finale reads relics, levels, allies, corruption, choices and secret items. It outputs boss phases, ending route and postgame state.

nodes.title: UI: @{UI Level Trail}
nodes.body: Vertical level track from 1 to 100 mirrors this node column. Shows next zone, required level, recommended sidequest prep and missing key tags.

nodes.title: UI: Title Screen Mood
nodes.body: A tiny shaking candle beneath a grey sky. As story progress rises, the logo gains sun, moon and root rings.

nodes.title: UI: @{UI Quest Web}
nodes.body: Quest journal is a node web: main path thick, side-sidequests thin, items and NPCs visibly connected to unlocks.

nodes.title: UI: @{UI Staff Rings}
nodes.body: Staff upgrade interface. Rings appear for @{Item Sunstone}, @{Item Moonpetal}, @{Item Earthsong}, null shard and secret prism.

nodes.title: UI: @{UI Corruption Map}
nodes.body: Shows creeping blight, restored tiles, lantern routes and ashstorm forecasts. Useful without spoiling hidden secrets.

nodes.title: UI: NPC Letters
nodes.body: Saved NPCs send short letters to @{Home Base} or @{Zone Warden Camp}. Emotional reward and subtle quest-state feedback.

nodes.title: UI: Gate Requirement Cards
nodes.body: Citadel gates show tags like @{Level 71-75}, @{Item Citadel Gate Key}, @{Item Ironbranch Sigil}, or high mercy/truth route.

nodes.title: UI: Boss Intro Cards
nodes.body: Each boss gets a symbol, title, one-line theme and arena rules. Browser-friendly spectacle without full cinematics.

nodes.title: UI: Final Ritual Screen
nodes.body: Player arranges relic rings, NPC voices and choice glyphs. The screen makes the final spell feel built, not selected.

nodes.title: Level 100 Capstone: World Guardian
nodes.body: At cap, the player has every core traversal spell, final staff form, postgame restoration tools and optional challenge versions of major bosses.

nodes.title: Ending: @{Ending Mercy}
nodes.body: @{Protagonist} breaks the perfection engine and releases the blight gently. Requires balanced relics, low corruption and many restored people.

nodes.title: New Game+ Hook: @{Unlock NG Plus}
nodes.body: A new constellation appears over @{Home Base}; echoes of choices change early zones and open harder alternate routes.

nodes.title: Postgame: @{Postgame}
nodes.body: Revisit zones to heal remaining scars, finish gardens, craft cosmetics, fight echoes and read consequence letters.

nodes.title: Ending: @{Ending Power}
nodes.body: @{Protagonist} controls the blight to protect @{World}. Strong, dramatic, but towns fear the new guardian. Triggered by heavy overcharge/control choices.

nodes.title: Secret Ending: @{Ending Secret}
nodes.body: Requires @{NPC Hollow Child} spared, @{Item Heartseed Prism}, strong ally flags and restored lanterns. The land answers back and changes the final boss into a healing dialogue.

nodes.title: Ending: @{Ending Truth}
nodes.body: @{Protagonist} reveals the wound that made @{Main Villain}. Some people forgive, some do not. Requires truth clues and Valerius memory quests.

nodes.title: Items

notes: Nieuwe gerichte game-node versie: het aangeleverde verhaal is niet gekopieerd als manuscript, maar vertaald naar speelbare progression, zones, quests, side-sidequests, NPCs, items, unlocks, mechanics, UI en level-gates van Level 1 tot Level 100. Tags zijn bewust vaak gebruikt voor namen van zones, NPCs, items, quests en levelranges zodat later hernoemen makkelijker blijft. Generated counts: 202 nodes, 365 edges, 146 tags, 460 tag mentions.

tags.name: Primary Theme
tags.value: fear becomes empathy, empathy becomes power

tags.name: Ending Power
tags.value: Ending: Crown of Root and Fire

tags.name: Ending Secret
tags.value: Secret Ending: The Land Answers Back
